title: Day 07 — Machine Coding Round 1
summary: 90-minute Week 1 review build: login, dashboard, sidebar, and navbar — from memory, under interview rules, with cumulative quiz and reflection.

blocks.content: This is not a new concepts day. This is the day product companies simulate — build a working mini-app in 90 minutes while interviewers watch architecture choices, debugging calm, and prioritization. You will rebuild login, dashboard, sidebar, and navbar without peeking at Week 1 solutions. Weak areas become your Week 2 focus list.

blocks.title: Rules — read before starting timer
blocks.content: No AI. No Google. No copy-paste from previous FrontForge code. Only official docs: react.dev, reactrouter.com, typescriptlang.org, vite.dev, developer.mozilla.org. Set a 90-minute timer. When it ends, stop and write your self-review.
Everything below was taught on its scheduled day. The machine round may combine them — that is intentional.

items.text: Day 01 — JSX, components, Vite, TypeScript props
Day 02 — useState, controlled forms, validation, events
Day 05 — React Router, Link, Outlet, nested layout, 404
Day 06 — custom hooks, debounce, responsive shell, theme (stretch)

rows: Vite + TS + Router shell, empty routes

blocks.title: Interview prioritization
blocks.content: A working ugly app beats a beautiful incomplete form. Navbar can be a single div. Sidebar can be a ul with Link. Placeholder page text is fine. Missing routing is not.

Build from scratch or an empty git branch. Simulated login: if email and password pass validation, navigate to /dashboard. Sidebar links to at least dashboard and profile. Navbar shows app name. Main area swaps via Outlet.

items.text: Sidebar + Navbar persist on dashboard routes
Two+ navigable sections via React Router

blocks.content: Can you scaffold components without copying? PascalCase, typed props, separate files.
Controlled login, preventDefault, validation before navigate.
Optional stretch: fetch user in profile page. If added, show loading and error — not required for minimum pass.
Nested layout with Outlet. Custom hooks optional bonus. Explain choices in interview section after build.

blocks.text: After the timer — review weak areas

blocks.content: Identify gaps honestly. Week 2 assumes you can build small React apps independently. If routing slowed you down, redo only DashboardLayout tomorrow morning for 30 minutes — do not repeat all seven days.

blocks.title: Week 1 complete
blocks.content: When quiz, interview, reflection, and timed build are done, you have finished Week 1 of FrontForge. Week 2 starts with API-driven Weather Dashboard on Day 08.
